## Overview

The forgetting curve describes how memory retention decays over time without reinforcement. Discovered by Hermann Ebbinghaus in 1885 through self-experimentation with nonsense syllables, the curve shows steep early loss followed by a long flat tail.

The forgetting curve is one of the most replicated findings in memory research and underpins the spaced repetition technique used in modern flashcard systems.
